name: Erbe-Verb
description: Modeless continuously-variable stereo reverb, co-designed with Tom Erbe (SoundHack). All seven parameters under CV control; Size sweeps coffin-to-heavens without algorithm boundaries. Syncable reverse.

controls.name: Level
controls.description: Input gain. Unity at 3:00. Past 3:00 introduces gain and possible overdrive.

controls.name: Mix
controls.description: Dry/wet blend. Acts as attenuator for Mix CV when patched.

controls.name: Size
controls.description: Large grey knob. Sets size of virtual space, coffin to heavens. Defining parameter.
controls.detail: 35 cu ft – 9.3 million cu ft · CCW: coffin · 12: room · 1-2: plate · 3: hall · 4: ambient · CW: heaven

controls.name: Speed
controls.description: Speed of internal modulation. Has no effect when Depth at noon.
controls.detail: internal clock: 0.5 – 256 cps · ext clock: 1/48 – 9000 cps

controls.name: Pre-Delay
controls.description: Time before first reflections. Also sets Reverse buffer length.
controls.detail: internal: 7ms – 500ms · ext clk: bipolar divisors 1/12–12/1 (1/1 at noon) · reverse: 42ms–500ms int / 0.1s–5.46s ext

controls.name: Absorb
controls.description: Combined diffusion and damping. Feedback-based — slow response.
controls.detail: CCW: 0 diffusion, 0 damping · 10:00: full diffusion, 0 damping · CW: full diffusion + damping

controls.name: Absorb CV
controls.description: Direct CV input for Absorb. No attenuator.

controls.name: Depth
controls.description: Bipolar. Sets amount and type of internal modulation. 12:00 = off.
controls.detail: CCW: Cyclic (chorus) · noon: off · CW: Ergodic (granular) · full CW: Shimmer

controls.name: Tilt
controls.description: Bipolar tone tilt. Last stage — independent of reverb energy.
controls.detail: CCW: +12 dB low / -12 dB high · noon: flat · CW: -24 dB low / +24 dB high

controls.name: Decay
controls.description: Unipolar. Reverb tail length. 100% = unity (infinite); past 100% = self-oscillating feedback.

controls.name: Reverse
controls.description: Button toggles Reverse on/off. Reverses the Pre-Delay buffer for reverse reverb.
controls.detail: button · Reverse LED flickers at buffer length

inputs.name: Signal In
inputs.description: AC-coupled mono input. Unity ~3:00 on Level.

inputs.name: Mix CV
inputs.description: Unipolar Mix CV. Mix knob becomes attenuator when patched.

inputs.name: Pre-Delay CV
inputs.description: Unity-gain CV for Pre-Delay. No attenuator — use external VCA/MATHS channel for attenuation. Audio-rate yields metallic tones.

inputs.name: Absorb CV
inputs.description: Direct CV for Absorb. No attenuator.

inputs.name: Tempo
inputs.description: External clock sync. Pre-Delay and Speed follow divisions/multiples. Needs ≥1.5V, ≥6ms wide.

outputs.name: L (Mono) Out
outputs.description: Left channel — also serves as mono output.

outputs.name: CV Out
outputs.description: Envelope follower of reverb energy. Rises as reverb signal gets louder. Useful for feedback automation (patch to Decay/Absorb CV).

behaviors.name: Size as continuous space
behaviors.description: Unlike reverbs with algorithm modes, Size sweeps continuously through coffin→room→plate→hall→ambient→heaven. Modulating Size dramatically morphs the virtual space — slow modulation crossfades between rooms, fast modulation produces metallic pitch-shift artifacts.

behaviors.name: Pre-Delay clock sync
behaviors.description: With Tempo In patched, Pre-Delay control becomes bipolar selector over divisors/multipliers (1/12, 1/8, 1/6, 1/4, 1/3, 1/2, 2/3, 1/1, 3/2, 2/1, 3/1, 4/1, 6/1, 8/1, 12/1) with 1/1 at noon. Speed also follows the incoming clock.

behaviors.name: Reverse buffer
behaviors.description: Plays the Pre-Delay buffer backwards for reverse-reverb. Reverse buffer size = Pre-Delay setting: 42ms–500ms internal, 0.1s–5.46s when synced. Gate-momentary for rhythmic reverse effects.

behaviors.name: Depth polarity (Cyclic vs Ergodic)
behaviors.description: Bipolar Depth chooses type of internal modulation. CCW from noon: Cyclic — multi-phase chorus, subtle to doppler-swirl. CW from noon: Ergodic — random room dimension shifting, becoming granular. Full CW: 'Shimmer'. Note: at noon Speed has no audible effect.

behaviors.name: Infinite feedback past unity
behaviors.description: Decay is feedback-based. Past unity (100%) the reverb self-sustains / drones indefinitely. Useful for freeze/infinite halls — press/hold a gate to Decay CV (via attenuator inversion) to punch into infinite feedback.

behaviors.name: Absorb feedback response
behaviors.description: Absorb is feedback-based and slower than Size/Pre-Delay. Use slow envelopes, LFOs, or clocked Pressure Points sequences for best audible effect. Greater impact when Decay is higher. Full CCW = no diffusion, 0 damping; 10:00 = full diffusion, 0 damping; full CW = full + full damping.

behaviors.name: Tilt as final tone
behaviors.description: Tilt is the last operation in the algorithm — does not affect energy or feedback. Bipolar EQ tilt like a rocking see-saw. Slow Tilt modulation with long decay gives phase-shifting character.

behaviors.name: CV Out envelope-follower
behaviors.description: CV Out tracks the reverb signal loudness. Patch to Decay CV (with attenuverter inversion) for an automatic 'reigning in' of feedback before it runs away. Patch to Size CV for space-reshaping based on density. Patch outside Erbe-Verb to open other modules with reverb tails.

behaviors.name: Pre-Delay audio-rate FM
behaviors.description: Pre-Delay accepts audio-rate CV without an attenuator. Modulating at audio rates produces metallic, clangorous tones. Attenuate through a MATHS channel first or the effect can be overwhelming.

patchIdeas.name: Gated reverb
patchIdeas.patch: Mult source → Signal In AND → MATHS CH 1 Trig · MATHS Rise = 12:00, Fall full CCW (linear response) · MATHS CH 1 OUT → Mix CV · Mix knob sets max wetness (attenuates the CV) · each trigger opens the reverb then slams it shut — 80s drum-gate sound.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Erbe-Verb:
* Size: ~2:00 (plate)
* Decay: 12:00

- Source (Out) -> Mult (In)
- Mult (Out 1) -> Erbe-Verb (Signal In)
- Mult (Out 2) g> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> Erbe-Verb (Mix CV) // gated wet
- Erbe-Verb (L) -> Output (L)
- Erbe-Verb (R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Decay Hell (infinite feedback on demand)
patchIdeas.patch: Sine voice → Optomix CH 1 (plucked via MATHS cycle) → Signal In · Start from Hall settings · Pressure Points Pressure Out → Decay CV (attenuator full CW) · pressing a PP plate pushes Decay into infinite feedback, releasing returns to hall · hands-on feedback performance.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Erbe-Verb:
* Size: 3:00 (hall)
* Decay: just below unity
* Decay CV Att: full CW

- STO (SUB) -> Optomix.CH 1 (Signal In)
- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> Optomix.CH 1 (Strike)
- Optomix.CH 1 (Out) -> Erbe-Verb (Signal In)
- Pressure Points (Pressure) >> Erbe-Verb (Decay CV) // push to infinity
- Erbe-Verb (L) -> Output (L)

patchIdeas.name: Synced reverse slapback
patchIdeas.patch: Clock → Tempo In · Reverse ON (button) · Pre-Delay set for 1/2 · clock-aligned reverse slapback on every note · modulate Size with slow LFO for shifting reverse-room character · patch clock to Speed via Tempo too for a musical internal modulation rhythm.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Erbe-Verb:
* Size: ~12:00 (room)
* Pre-Delay: 1/2 (noon-ish)
* Reverse: ON

- Clock (Out) c> Erbe-Verb (Tempo)
- Source (Out) -> Erbe-Verb (Signal In)
- LFO (Out) >> Erbe-Verb (Size CV)
- Erbe-Verb (L) -> Output (L)
- Erbe-Verb (R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Shimmer drone
patchIdeas.patch: Size full CW · Pre-Delay full CW · Decay 2:00 · Depth full CW (Shimmer, Ergodic max) · Speed 12:00–3:00 · drone source → Signal In · Mix 100% · upward shimmer octave-cascades on sustained tones · slow CV on Tilt keeps motion fresh.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Erbe-Verb:
* Size: full CW (heaven)
* Pre-Delay: full CW
* Decay: 2:00
* Depth: full CW (Shimmer)
* Mix: 100% wet

- Drone (Out) -> Erbe-Verb (Signal In)
- Slow LFO (Out) >> Erbe-Verb (Tilt CV)
- Erbe-Verb (L) -> Output (L)
- Erbe-Verb (R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Self-tuning feedback via CV Out
patchIdeas.patch: Any source → Signal In · Decay set just past noon · CV Out → MATHS CH 2 In (attenuverter full CCW to invert) · CH 2 Out → Decay CV · as reverb grows, CV Out opens MATHS inverter which clamps Decay down · automatic runaway-prevention lets you sit near feedback edge safely.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Erbe-Verb:
* Decay: ~1:00
* Decay CV Att: to taste

- Source (Out) -> Erbe-Verb (Signal In)
- Erbe-Verb (CV Out) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) >> Erbe-Verb (Decay CV) // self-regulation (invert CH 2)
- Erbe-Verb (L) -> Output (L)

patchIdeas.name: Pre-Delay metallic FM
patchIdeas.patch: Source → Signal In · audio-rate sine VCO → MATHS CH 2 attenuverter → Pre-Delay CV · Size 12:00 · Decay 1:00 · audio-rate modulation of Pre-Delay produces clangorous metallic tones that scale with modulation frequency — use attenuation to dial in FM index.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Erbe-Verb:
* Size: 12:00
* Decay: 1:00

- Source (Out) -> Erbe-Verb (Signal In)
- VCO (Sine) -> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) >> Erbe-Verb (Pre-Delay CV) // audio-rate metal
- Erbe-Verb (L) -> Output (L)
- Erbe-Verb (R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Momentary reverse stabs
patchIdeas.patch: Drum source → Signal In · Size 1:00 (plate) · Pre-Delay 11:00 · Mix 50% · snare hits also gate → Reverse Gate · each snare triggers a short reverse burst of its own tail · pre-delay CV from sequencer randomizes buffer-length reverse lengths.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Erbe-Verb:
* Size: 1:00 (plate)
* Pre-Delay: 11:00
* Mix: 50%

- Drums (Out) -> Mult (In)
- Mult (Out 1) -> Erbe-Verb (Signal In)
- Mult (Out 2) g> Erbe-Verb (Reverse Gate)
- Sequencer (CV) >> Erbe-Verb (Pre-Delay CV)
- Erbe-Verb (L) -> Output (L)
- Erbe-Verb (R) -> Output (R)
